definition: Represent each variant by the set of mutated positions, or by position:amino-acid tokens when substitution identity matters, then compute Jaccard similarity to training variants. This audits whether performance depends on seeing the same sites or substitutions during training.
example_usage: python set_jaccard_similarity.py --train train_variants.csv --eval eval_variants.csv --out pairwise_similarity.csv --set-col mutated_positions

citations.title: Investigating the determinants of performance in machine learning for protein fitness prediction

evidence.summary: The paper defines positional extrapolation as predicting mutation effects at sites or amino acids not exposed during training.
The experiments train with a site fixed and test on alternative amino acids at that site to measure positional extrapolation.

limitations: Position overlap ignores sequence background and long-range epistasis.
A position-only set is too coarse when substitution identity is central.
Requires a consistent coordinate system across variants.
quality_gates: Choose position-only or position:amino-acid tokens before running the audit.
Report how wild-type/reference residues were assigned.
Run a separate sequence-distance curve when background sequence context varies.
